notes: Codex reset (`/new`) supersession. Codex's Source is FilesUnderRoot(~/.codex/sessions): a proc-<PID> pre-session appears at launch, hands off to a rollout-keyed UUID-1 session on the first prompt, runs one turn, then the reset (driven here via the reset_session step) + a second prompt spawns a SECOND rollout (UUID-2) with a fresh session_id and no UUID-1 history. The reset's user-observable signal is v2_session_birth (new_session:true) — a rollout-keyed session_id never seen before; the new session superseding the old IS the expected behavior. The driver's reset_session step + multi-session session.uuids capture BOTH rollouts, so the curated fixture contains UUID-1 and UUID-2 and these phases validate 8/8 with no drift. No transcript_removed fires at the reset keystroke ITSELF, but only because reset is lazy (the new rollout appears on the first post-reset prompt); the old rollout FILE then lingers frozen on disk. The removal IS produced — both rollouts share one PID, so when the new rollout's PID is discovered the daemon's same-PID cleanup (#169, the SAME path as claudecode) emits transcript_removed for the old UUID (seq 1602, ~7ms after the new pid_discovered — NOT a readyTTL sweep). There is deliberately NO old_session_ended phase: a fixture scoping choice (v2_session_birth is the canonical reset proxy), not because codex never produces a removal.

text: Pre-session row (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 1s of codex launch
After the first prompt lands, the rollout-keyed session appears in ready — proc-<PID> hands off to a fresh UUID
First user prompt drives ready → working on the rollout session
First turn completes back to ready (same rollout UUID)

Spec: 'New session appears in ready with a NEW session_id' — after /clear, the first post-clear prompt spawns a fresh rollout UUID never seen before; this is the strongest observable proxy for 'the conversation was reset'
Second user prompt drives the NEW session ready → working (must NOT match a stale working transition on UUID-1)
Second turn completes back to ready on the new session
